violations.text: Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Eggs and Brussels sprouts were placed hot in the refrigerator in plastic-wrapped containers greater than four inches deep. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/15/2025
Water observed in stacked food containers. After cleaning and sanitizing, equipment and utensils must be air dried. New Violation. Correct By: 12/15/2025
Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the dry storage area. New Violation. Correct By: 12/15/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (knives) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/12/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the (prep tables) have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 12/12/2024
Inspector observed leak at pipes under the ware washing area. Per PIC, work order was submitted. New Violation. Correct By: 12/12/2024

The ceilings throughout the kitchen and warewashing have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 12/12/2024

Food contact and non-food contact surfaces of can opener and ice machine were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 11/27/2023

Soup, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Soup was observed in walk-in freezer at 85-94 degrees Fahrenheit, per interview soup was prepared 2 hours ago. PIC reheated soup to 165 degrees. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/27/2023

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings throughout the kitchen and office areas. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 11/27/2023
